```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(GenomicRanges)
library(tidyverse)
library(pheatmap)
packageVersion("GenomicRanges")
packageVersion("pheatmap")
```

```{r}
# --- 1. SETTINGS & PATHS ---
macs_peaks_dir <- "/nfs/turbo/umms-hammou/mrabbani/DATA_STORAGE/ATAC-processed/Tim_Parnell_analysis/Mashiat_analysis/ATAC_alldatasets/macs_peaks_all_atac"
atac_rds_path  <- '/nfs/turbo/umms-hammou/mrabbani/DATA_STORAGE/ATAC-processed/Tim_Parnell_analysis/Mashiat_analysis/MDI_downloads/new_mdi/protAtacTss.gcrz_obs.rds'

stages <- c("early_RS", "int_RS", "late_RS", "earliest_ES", "early_ES", "int_ES", "late_ES")
signal_cols <- stages # Adjust if your columns have "_signal" suffixes
```

```{r}
# --- 2. CREATE UNION PEAKSET ---
# Use a named vector to read and label files in one go
peak_files <- setNames(
  paste0(macs_peaks_dir, "/all_", stages, "_narrowpeaks.sorted.merged.bed"),
  stages
)

union_peaks_gr <- map_df(names(peak_files), function(stg) {
  read.table(peak_files[stg], sep="\t") %>%
    select(chrom=1, start=2, end=3)
}) %>%
  makeGRangesFromDataFrame() %>%
  GenomicRanges::reduce() # Merges overlapping regions

# Add a unique ID for tracking
set.seed(42)
# We pick 65k to ensure we have at least 50k left after drop_na()
union_peaks_gr <- sample(union_peaks_gr, min(length(union_peaks_gr), 65000))
union_peaks_gr$peak_id <- paste0("peak_", seq_along(union_peaks_gr))
#union_peaks_gr$peak_id <- paste0("peak_", seq_along(union_peaks_gr))
```

```{r}
# check the late ES atac scores at peak_11988 from union_peaks_gr
peak_11988 <- union_peaks_gr[union_peaks_gr$peak_id == "peak_11988"]
print(peak_11988)

# look for the corresponding bin in the atac_data
#atac_data <- readRDS(atac_rds_path)
bin_11988 <- atac_data %>%
  filter(chrom == as.character(seqnames(peak_11988)) &
         start0 <= end(peak_11988) & end1 >= start(peak_11988))
print(bin_11988)

```

```{r}
# --- 3. LOAD ATAC BINS & AGGREGATE ---
atac_data <- readRDS(atac_rds_path)

# Convert to GRanges directly from the object
bin_gr <- atac_data %>%
  # Use the actual column names from your head() output
  dplyr::rename(start = start0, end = end1) %>%
  makeGRangesFromDataFrame(keep.extra.columns = TRUE)

# Find overlaps: Query (Union Peaks) vs Subject (1kb Bins)
ov <- findOverlaps(union_peaks_gr, bin_gr)

# Aggregate signal: Map bin values to peaks and calculate Mean
peak_signals <- as.data.frame(mcols(bin_gr)[subjectHits(ov), ]) %>%
  # Attach the peak_id from the union_peaks query
  mutate(peak_id = union_peaks_gr$peak_id[queryHits(ov)]) %>%
  group_by(peak_id) %>%
  # Calculate mean signal and mean stageType for the merged region
  #summarise(across(all_of(c(signal_cols, "stageType")), mean, na.rm = TRUE)) %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(signal_cols), mean, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop") %>%
  # 2. Only keep rows with complete data
  drop_na(all_of(signal_cols)) #%>%
  # CALCULATE STAGE_MEAN
  # We use pmax(., 0) to ensure negative residuals don't break the weighted average
  # 2. CENTER THE STAGES (The Fix)
# This subtracts 0.57 from early_RS, 0.96 from late_ES, etc.
centered_signals <- peak_signals %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(signal_cols), ~ .x - mean(.x, na.rm = TRUE)))

# 3. CALCULATE STAGE_MEAN (stgm)
# We use the centered values. We use pmax(., 0) because 
# a weighted average requires non-negative weights.
peak_signals <- centered_signals %>%
  mutate(
    total_pos = rowSums(across(all_of(signal_cols), ~ pmax(.x, 0))),
    stage_mean = ifelse(total_pos > 0, (
      (pmax(early_RS, 0) * 1) + 
      (pmax(int_RS, 0) * 2) + 
      (pmax(late_RS, 0) * 3) + 
      (pmax(earliest_ES, 0) * 4) + 
      (pmax(early_ES, 0) * 5) + 
      (pmax(int_ES, 0) * 6) + 
      (pmax(late_ES, 0) * 7)
    ) / total_pos, NA)
  ) %>%
  drop_na(stage_mean) %>% # Remove peaks with no positive signal across all stages
  # Now trim to your exact target
  slice_head(n = 50000)
```

```{r}
# --- 4. SUBSAMPLE & ORDER ---
set.seed(42)
final_data <- peak_signals %>%
  arrange(stage_mean) %>% # Order rows by stage_mean value
  column_to_rownames("peak_id")

# Create matrix for heatmap (removing stage_mean column from the plot itself)
mat_ordered <- final_data %>% 
  select(all_of(signal_cols)) %>% 
  as.matrix()

is_dynamic <- apply(mat_ordered, 1, function(x) max(x) - min(x) > 1.0)
mat_filtered <- mat_ordered[is_dynamic, ]

nrow(mat_filtered)
```

```{r}
# group by signal col and calculate mean value for each stage
group_means <- final_data %>%
  summarise(across(all_of(signal_cols), mean, na.rm = TRUE))
print(group_means)
```




```{r}
pdf("figure_plots/stagetype_distribution.pdf", width = 6, height = 8)
hist(final_data$stage_mean, breaks = 7, main = "Distribution of stage_mean values", xlab = "stage_mean")
dev.off()
```

```{r}
# --- 5. PLOT ---
pdf("figure_plots/atac_macs_heatmap_filtered.pdf", width = 6, height = 8)
pheatmap(
  mat_filtered,
  cluster_rows = FALSE,  # Keep our stage_mean ordering
  cluster_cols = FALSE,  # Keep stage progression order
  scale = "row",         # Z-score normalization
  show_rownames = FALSE,
  color = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(100),
  main = "Subsampled ATAC Signal (Ordered by Stage Mean)",
  breaks = seq(-3, 3, length.out = 101) # <-- sets color scale from -3 to 3
)
dev.off()
```
